_comment: GENERIC EXAMPLE — all values are fictional placeholders for a made-up brand. Replace EVERYTHING with the real brand's SolCrys data and fresh research. Do not ship these numbers. The `body` array is an ordered list of blocks; the builder renders them in order. Block types: {h1},{h2},{p,bold?,italic?},{note},{bullets:[]},{numbers:[]},{table:{cols,rows,widths?,highlightLastCol?}},{sources:[[label,url]],sourcesTitle?},{pageBreak},{space}.

meta.title: Winning the AI Answer Layer
meta.subtitle: A 3-Month Content & AEO Proposal to Close the Visibility Gap with [Leader Brand]
meta.goalLine: Goal: lift [Brand]'s AI share of voice from 27% to 40% over 12 weeks
meta.preparedFor: Prepared for: Office of the CEO, [Brand]
meta.evidenceBase: Evidence base: SolCrys AEO platform — [window], [N] tracked prompts, 4 engines (ChatGPT, Gemini, Google AIO, Perplexity)
meta.header: [Brand] — Winning the AI Answer Layer | CEO Proposal

body.p: Buyers increasingly ask AI assistants to recommend [category] software. [Leader] is named in [Y]% of relevant answers; [Brand] in [X]%, ranking #[r] of [n]. Category discovery is moving to AI and the leader is compounding its lead.

This is a content-supply problem, not a perception one: when [Brand] is named, sentiment is strongly positive and it already ranks #[k] on 'primary' recommendations. AI engines can only cite what exists, and [Brand]'s content footprint is thin across owned (1P), editorial (3P) and UGC layers.

Why three months: publishing is not citation. Perplexity reflects new content in ~2–4 weeks and Google AI Overviews in ~2–8 weeks, but ChatGPT runs a 6–12 week lag. We front-load production into Month 1, then use Months 2–3 as deliberate pickup buffer so results are real and measured fairly.

body.h1: 1. Key Takeaways from the SolCrys AEO Data

body.p: Across [total] citations the engines pulled when answering [Brand]'s [N] category prompts, here is where the brand stands by content layer.

rows: On a couple of authority sites
Absent from the top community domain

body.p: Priority gaps: (1) UGC blind spot; (2) industry-layer absence; (3) owned thinness.

rows: Build breadth of citable owned pages (refresh-first)
Lean on the brand's own moats

body.h2: 1.3  Category citation insights (based on current prompts)

body.p: Sourcing mix: [i]% industry/other, [u]% UGC, [e]% editorial, [o]% owned. UGC and editorial are highly concentrated — a few domains carry almost all of it.

body.note: Insight: UGC is effectively one community site; editorial is thin and concentrated — both are short, addressable lists.

sources: SolCrys citation insights by source type & domain

body.h1: 2. The Indexing & Pickup Lag Model (why 3 months + buffer)

body.p: Before an engine can cite a page it must crawl, index/rank, then select it into an answer. Crawling is fast; the lag is in ranking and selection, and it differs by engine. Treat these as planning estimates, not guarantees.

bullets: Earned media adds ~2–6 weeks pitch-to-publish before the engine clock starts — pitch early.
UGC needs to age and gather engagement before engines treat it as citable — start early.

body.p: Implication: front-load owned content into Month 1 so the ChatGPT clock starts immediately; treat Months 2–3 as the pickup buffer, not downtime. A 2-month plan tends to measure before ChatGPT reflects the work.

Of [N] prompts, [Brand] is invisible (0 mentions) on [a], weak on [b], and leads on [c]. SOV won't move in a straight line — expect flat-to-modest in Month 1, acceleration in Month 2, target in Month 3.

rows: Content live + indexed; Perplexity first pickups
Perplexity + Google AIO reflect M1 content; earned media live
ChatGPT catches up; UGC + earned compounding

body.note: [Weakest engine] is the biggest early-win lever and the clearest leading indicator the plan is working.

body.p: Goal: 100% of owned content live and crawlable by week 4; start long-lead earned-media pitches now.

bullets: Reconcile conflicting facts (Appendix A); confirm AI crawlers unblocked; submit sitemaps.
Refresh existing owned pages into answer-shaped, citable form: [/existing-page-a], [/existing-page-b] (already cited but not answer-shaped); add Product/FAQ schema where it earns rich results.
Create only the gap pages — where the inventory found no existing coverage: [/new-page] for invisible prompt '[prompt]', plus a comparison hub vs [Leader].
Publish 2 verified case studies; start editorial pitches and disclosed Reddit/LinkedIn presence.

body.note: Closes owned thinness + invisible prompts. Starts the ChatGPT (6–12wk) and Perplexity (2–4wk) clocks. KPI: owned AEO score up; pages indexed.

bullets: Earned media publishes; pursue inclusion in the roundups engines already cite; case studies syndicated; trade PR.
YouTube explainers; sustain Reddit answers + a writeup; weekly LinkedIn from named execs; analyst briefings.

body.note: Closes editorial halo + industry-layer absence. Perplexity + Google AIO pickup of M1 content. KPI: owned-citation share up; weakest engine up.

bullets: ChatGPT begins reflecting M1 content; double down on still-lagging prompts; secure tier-1 feature.
Final amplification; refresh top pages for freshness; full SolCrys read vs target; set ongoing cadence.

body.note: ChatGPT catch-up + cross-engine freshness. KPI: SOV → target; invisible prompts 0.

rows: Fact fix; refresh existing owned pages; create gap-only pages; comparison hub; case studies; start pitches
Earned media; roundup inclusions; PR; YouTube; Reddit; exec LinkedIn
Refresh lagging pages; tier-1 feature; final push; measure; set cadence

body.note: SOV is a lagging metric — track leading indicators (pages indexed, bots crawling, first Perplexity pickups) in Month 1.

body.p: Reconcile conflicting brand claims (engines surface the weakest) and flag competitor claims unsupported by independent sources.
The brand's citable, verified results — specific numbers beat vague ranges.
Vetted against competitors: each outlet is one a tracked competitor is already cited from (a proven channel the brand is missing). Speculative outlets are labeled as such and ranked last.

sources: SolCrys citation data — competitor presence by domain

body.p: The 'best [category]' roundups engines already cite that omit the brand — proven because competitors are already on the list.

The top community domain feeds ChatGPT + Google AIO; the brand is absent. Priority is set by competitor presence — communities where a competitor is already cited or recommended rank HIGH. Disclosed, helpful participation by a named expert — not promotion. Counts approximate; verify live.

bullets: Disclose affiliation; help-first; ~1-in-10 self-promo ratio; never post confidential data.
Starter posts built from the Proof Library (Appendix B).
Weekly: 1 results post (Appendix B stat) + 1 explainer, posted from named execs — personal profiles are more citable than the company page.
Repurpose every earned-media piece as a post to create another citable URL.

body.p: Verification note: indexing windows are planning estimates that vary by domain authority and competition. Vendor figures are company claims unless independently sourced. SolCrys figures are from the '[slug]' workspace, [window], [N] prompts, 4 engines. Confirm community counts and outlet terms live before committing budget.
